The Court stated that it had reviewed the papers. Mr. Der stated that the parties have ongoing discussions regarding settling this matter and that the parties were requesting a settlement conference with the Court. The Court inquired about mediation. Mr. Strauss stated that they weren't opposed to a settlement conference or mediation if there isn't an expense. The Court stated that it appeared that the Trust qualifies for Pro Bono mediation. Mr. Feder stated that he had no objection to either mediation or a settlement conference. The Court stated that that it sounded as if all of the parties agree to some form of ADR and that it would focus on mediation. Additional discussions regarding mediation were held. Mr. Feder suggested that the parties do Pro Bono mediation, that the parties continue to talk, and stated that if there were to be an evidentiary hearing that he didn't think the settlement conference should be done here. The Court stated that it would not set a judicial settlement conference but it was referring the matter to Pro Bono mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 7, 2011. Status on mediation is set for January 10, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jay Strauss for Amir Izadpanah (Ojector). Ernest Der for John Kohler. Bruce Feder for Conservatee. Dennis Hyde for Jonathan Lewis.

Mr. Strauss stated that there was an offer made by Amir to pay $1,200.00 a month as of now going forward. The Court asked for information regarding Amir's salary, any and all properties owned by him in the world, his rents and his expenses. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Amir not meeting his obligations to the note, breach of contract, the TIC (Tenants in Common) agreement, Amir's failure to convert the property into condominiums and Amir's failure to complete the application for condo conversion. Mr. Strauss stated that Amir is in distress, that Amir intends to fully pay the obligation when he returns in Spring of 2011 and that he is willing to pay what he can. Mr. Feder stated that he would like Amir to pay what he can without the surrendering of any other rights. The Court asked Mr. Strauss' Client to consider filing the condo conversion application. The Court denied the request to impose an equitable lien against the Pacific Avenue property and will not rule on any other relief requested. The matter was continued to December 6, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if Amir can come up with any funds. The Court wants information regarding Amir's return to the United States and his employer's position. Anything that is to be filed should be filed by December 1, 2010. Responses are due by December 2, 2010.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Continued To Nov-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jay Strauss for Amir Izadapaneh. Dennis Hyde for and with Jonathan Lewis (Objector). Ernest Der for and with John Kohler IV. Bruce Feder for Judy Kohler.

Mr. Strauss presented opposition to the petition stating that the property sold for the full asking price, that the contract specifically addresses the event of a default, that there will be foreclosure on the Hermosa property, and that there will be no liens. In addition, he stated that his client was moved to Germany by his job, that his client may return next year, that his client offered to pay $1,000 per month, that he offered to give them the Hermosa property, and that they could then foreclose/sell the property. Mr Hyde stated that his client feels like he is in the middle, that he keeps getting drug back into court, and that as far as he is concerned everything has been done to go ahead with the condo conversion. Mr. Feder stated that there doesn't seem to be any reason to have the hold up, that it appears that this is a delay tactic, and requested that the Court require Mr. Izadapaneh to commence the application for conversion process forthwith. Mr. Strauss stated that his client cannot afford to pay on the note, that his client had to establish a household in Germany, confirmed that his client receives rent from the Hermosa property which is used to pay the underlying mortgage, that he didn't know about rent on the Manhattan Beach property, and that there may be other properties in Europe. The Court stated that it wants to know Mr. Izadapaneh's income, if he is receiving rent from the Hermosa property, if he has other homes, how much his rent in Germany is, and about the Manhattan property. Mr. Strauss' opposition, if any, is to be filed by 11/8/10 and any reply is to be filed by the end of business on 11/10/10. The Court continued the matter to November 15, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda.
